post.description:
Execute a workflow to create an image generation.

This endpoint:
1. Retrieves the workflow and specified version (or default version)
2. Applies prompt modifications (addition/deletion) to the user's prompt
3. Merges workflow config with user overrides
4. Creates a generation record using the workflow's model

Recommend workflows based on user instruction and additional info using LLM.

This endpoint uses an LLM to analyze the user's requirements and recommend
the most appropriate workflows. Falls back to random selection if LLM fails.

Args:
    instruction: User's description of what they want to create
    additional_info: Additional context (e.g., asset type, style, etc.)
    count: Number of recommendations to return (1-10, default 3)

Returns:
    A list of recommended workflows with their default versions

Upload a workflow sample image to Supabase storage.
Returns the public URL to be used in workflow sample_images array.

File restrictions:
- Image types only (png, jpg, jpeg, webp, gif)
- Max size: 10MB

post.description:
Extract asset information from file content using LLM

This endpoint takes text content and uses an LLM to extract structured asset information.
The LLM analyzes the content and returns a list of assets with their properties.

Batch create assets from ImageAssetCreateRequest objects

This endpoint takes a list of ImageAssetCreateRequest objects and creates them
as ImageAssets in the database.

Generate an optimized prompt for image asset creation using LLM

This endpoint takes asset information and generator specifications,
then uses an LLM to create an optimized generation prompt.

Generate an image using external API and return raw image bytes (quota enforced)

This is a standalone endpoint that generates an image and returns it directly
without storing it in the asset system.

get.description: Get a single mutation brief by ID

post.description: Create a new mutation brief

Start an evaluation on a project's game design knowledge base.

This endpoint returns immediately with a pending evaluation record.
The actual evaluation runs asynchronously in the background.

Use the GET /{project_id}/evaluate/{evaluation_id} endpoint to poll
for the evaluation status and retrieve results once completed.

The evaluation analyzes the project's KB and produces a structured report
including gaps, risks, market analysis, and greenlight status.

get.description:
Get evaluation history for a project.

Returns a list of past evaluations ordered by most recent first.

Get the most recent evaluation for a project.

Returns 404 if no evaluations exist for the project.

Get a specific evaluation by ID.

Returns 404 if the evaluation doesn't exist or doesn't belong to the project.

post.description:
Ask a question about the project and get a structured Q&A response.

Supports both authenticated (JWT) and external (passcode) access.

The response includes:
- Answer or explicit acknowledgment of uncertainty
- Source references
- Confidence level
- Escalation info (if needed)

Ask a question about the project and get a structured Q&A response.

Supports both authenticated (JWT) and external (passcode) access.

The response includes:
- Answer or explicit acknowledgment of uncertainty
- Source references
- Confidence level
- Escalation info (if needed)

get.description:
Get all available subscription plans.
Public endpoint - no authentication required.

Get current user's subscription details.
Requires authentication.

post.description:
Activate or upgrade subscription using either a discount code or plan_id.

- If discount_code is provided: Activates subscription using the discount code (plan determined by code)
- If plan_id is provided: Activates subscription directly for the specified plan (for paid subscriptions)

Exactly one of discount_code or plan_id must be provided.
